constraints.name: Requirement_invariant_1
constraints.rule: requirementCode must be unique

constraints.name: Requirement_invariant_2
constraints.rule: priority must be one of MoSCoW values: MUST | SHOULD | COULD | WONT

constraints.name: Requirement_invariant_3
constraints.rule: A requirement must have at least one stakeholder source

constraints.name: FunctionalRequirement_invariant_1
constraints.rule: Must define clear acceptance criteria

constraints.name: FunctionalRequirement_invariant_2
constraints.rule: Must specify expected system behavior

constraints.name: NonFunctionalRequirement_invariant_1
constraints.rule: Must specify measurable quality criteria

constraints.name: NonFunctionalRequirement_invariant_2
constraints.rule: qualityAttribute must be one of: PERFORMANCE | SECURITY | USABILITY | RELIABILITY | MAINTAINABILITY

constraints.name: SecurityRequirement_invariant_1
constraints.rule: Must define a security level: LOW | MEDIUM | HIGH | CRITICAL

constraints.name: SecurityRequirement_invariant_2
constraints.rule: Must specify mitigation strategy for HIGH and CRITICAL security levels

constraints.name: SecurityRequirement_invariant_3
constraints.rule: Must be verified by a security-type TestCase

constraints.name: PerformanceRequirement_invariant_1
constraints.rule: Must specify a measurable metric and threshold

constraints.name: PerformanceRequirement_invariant_2
constraints.rule: unit must match the selected metric: ms|s for RESPONSE_TIME, MB|GB for MEMORY_USAGE, % for CPU_USAGE, req/s for THROUGHPUT

constraints.name: PerformanceRequirement_invariant_3
constraints.rule: Must be verified by a performance-type TestCase

constraints.name: Stakeholder_invariant_1
constraints.rule: stakeholderName must be unique

constraints.name: Stakeholder_invariant_2
constraints.rule: role must be defined and one of: DEVELOPER | ADMIN | TESTER | MANAGER | END_USER

constraints.name: Goal_invariant_1
constraints.rule: goalName must be unique

constraints.name: Goal_invariant_2
constraints.rule: A goal must be refined by at least one requirement

constraints.name: UseCase_invariant_1
constraints.rule: useCaseName must be unique

constraints.name: UseCase_invariant_2
constraints.rule: actor must map to an existing Stakeholder role

constraints.name: UseCase_invariant_3
constraints.rule: Must have at least one step in mainFlow

constraints.name: UseCase_invariant_4
constraints.rule: Must relate to at least one Requirement

constraints.name: SystemComponent_invariant_1
constraints.rule: componentName must be unique

constraints.name: TestCase_invariant_1
constraints.rule: testCaseCode must be unique

constraints.name: TestCase_invariant_2
constraints.rule: Must verify at least one requirement

constraints.name: TestCase_invariant_3
constraints.rule: expectedOutput must be defined

@graph.name: Requirement
@graph.description: A documented need or constraint for the REXX knowledge base system
invariant: requirementCode must be unique
priority must be one of MoSCoW values
A requirement must have at least one stakeholder source

@graph.name: FunctionalRequirement
@graph.description: Describes a specific behavior of the REXX knowledge base such as searching errors by name, loading JSON files and displaying solutions
invariant: Must define clear acceptance criteria
Must specify expected system behavior

@graph.name: NonFunctionalRequirement
@graph.description: Describes a quality attribute of the REXX knowledge base such as fast search response, usability of the Python program and maintainability of JSON files
invariant: Must specify measurable quality criteria
qualityAttribute must be one of defined values

@graph.name: SecurityRequirement
@graph.description: Defines security constraints for the REXX knowledge base such as preventing unauthorized modification of error JSON files in the Git repository
invariant: Must define a security level
Must specify mitigation strategy for HIGH and CRITICAL levels
Must be verified by a security-type TestCase

@graph.name: PerformanceRequirement
@graph.description: Specifies measurable performance constraints for the REXX knowledge base such as error search response time across all JSON files
invariant: Must specify a measurable metric and threshold
unit must match the selected metric
Must be verified by a performance-type TestCase

@graph.name: Stakeholder
@graph.description: An individual or group with interest in the REXX knowledge base such as a REXX developer looking up errors or an admin maintaining JSON files
invariant: stakeholderName must be unique
role must be defined

@graph.name: Goal
@graph.description: A high-level objective that requirements refine and support in the REXX knowledge base such as providing fast error lookup or maintaining accurate error data in Git
invariant: goalName must be unique
A goal must be refined by at least one requirement

@graph.name: UseCase
@graph.description: A scenario describing how a stakeholder interacts with the REXX knowledge base such as searching an error by name, adding a new error JSON file or pushing errors to GitHub
invariant: useCaseName must be unique
actor must map to an existing Stakeholder role
Must have at least one step in mainFlow
Must relate to at least one Requirement

@graph.name: SystemComponent
@graph.description: A module or component of the REXX knowledge base that realizes requirements such as search errors.py (Python search program) and errors/ (JSON error store)
invariant: componentName must be unique

@graph.name: TestCase
@graph.description: A test that verifies requirement implementation in the REXX knowledge base such as searching a known error, handling unknown input and validating JSON structure
invariant: testCaseCode must be unique
Must verify at least one requirement
expectedOutput must be defined

@graph.name: HasSource
@graph.description: Links a REXX knowledge base requirement to its originating stakeholder
postcondition: Requirement is associated with at least one Stakeholder
Stakeholder is recorded as source of the requirement

@graph.name: Refines
@graph.description: Establishes that a REXX knowledge base requirement refines a higher-level goal
postcondition: Requirement contributes to achieving the Goal
Goal is refined by one or more Requirements

@graph.name: DependsOn
@graph.description: Defines dependency between requirements — e.g. displaying a solution depends on loading the JSON file correctly
postcondition: Dependent requirement cannot be implemented before prerequisite

@graph.name: ConflictsWith
@graph.description: Identifies conflicting requirements in the REXX knowledge base that need resolution
precondition: Conflict must be documented with a reason
postcondition: Conflict is recorded for stakeholder resolution
Requirements cannot both be implemented without resolution

@graph.name: RealizedBy
@graph.description: Links a requirement to the system component that implements it — e.g. search requirement is realized by search errors.py
precondition: Requirement must be in APPROVED or IMPLEMENTED state
postcondition: SystemComponent realizes one or more Requirements

@graph.name: VerifiedBy
@graph.description: Associates a REXX knowledge base requirement with test cases that verify its implementation
precondition: Requirement must be in IMPLEMENTED state
postcondition: Requirement is verified by one or more TestCases

@graph.name: TriggeredBy
@graph.description: Associates a REXX knowledge base use case with the stakeholder who initiates it — e.g. a developer triggers the search error use case
postcondition: UseCase is associated with an initiating Stakeholder
Stakeholder is recorded as actor of the UseCase

@graph.name: Covers
@graph.description: Associates a test case with the use case scenario it covers — e.g. TC001 covers the search error by name use case
postcondition: UseCase scenario is covered by one or more TestCases
TestCase is linked to a verifiable use case flow

@graph.name: RequirementDraft
@graph.description: Requirement is being drafted and not yet approved

@graph.name: RequirementApproved
@graph.description: Requirement has been reviewed and approved for implementation

@graph.name: RequirementImplemented
@graph.description: Requirement has been implemented in the REXX knowledge base system

@graph.name: RequirementVerified
@graph.description: Requirement implementation has been tested and verified

@graph.name: RequirementRejected
@graph.description: Requirement has been rejected and will not be implemented
